description: RIGHT JOIN makes left-side NOT NULL columns nullable because unmatched rows produce NULLs

columns.name: name
columns.note: NOT NULL in schema but nullable due to RIGHT JOIN (left side)

name: left_side_becomes_nullable
query_sql:
-- @name GetOrdersWithOptionalUser
-- @returns :many
SELECT u.name, o.id FROM users u RIGHT JOIN orders o ON u.id = o.user_id
schema_sql: CREATE TABLE users (id SERIAL PRIMARY KEY, name TEXT NOT NULL, email TEXT NOT NULL, bio TEXT, age INTEGER, score NUMERIC, status TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'active', created_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now())
CREATE TABLE orders (id SERIAL PRIMARY KEY, user_id INTEGER NOT NULL REFERENCES users(id), total INTEGER NOT NULL, discount INTEGER, notes TEXT, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now())

sqlc_comparison.scythe_improvement: scythe correctly infers that left-side columns of a RIGHT JOIN become nullable
sqlc_comparison.sqlc_behavior: sqlc marks u.name as non-nullable based on schema NOT NULL constraint, ignoring RIGHT JOIN
